name: surgical_robot_export_telemetry_foreign_control_law
premise: Hospital system general counsel Noah Feld must decide whether to disable cloud telemetry on da Vinci-class robots after a new export-control rule flags overseas aggregation of de-identified kinematics as dual-use. CMO Dr. Nadia El-Masri argues telemetry improves remote proctoring for rural surgeons during complex cases tonight. Disabling may breach vendor support contracts; keeping may expose the system to Commerce penalties if packets route through embargoed regions.

agents.name: Noah Feld
agents.identity: I am general counsel interpreting BIS rules, vendor contracts, and criminal penalties for knowing exports of technical data.
agents.goal: I want compliance without stranding rural cases that depend on proctored telepresence tonight.

agents.name: Dr. Nadia El-Masri
agents.identity: I am the chief medical officer accountable for surgical outcomes across affiliated rural sites and for informed consent around data flows in robotic surgery.
agents.goal: I want proctoring preserved if counsel can certify routing and aggregation paths are domestic-only for this case block.

choices: I will disable all overseas-routable telemetry immediately and fly attending surgeons to rural sites for tonight's block, accepting flight delays and added cost.
I will keep telemetry active under a temporary domestic-only routing injunction filed with the vendor and accept personal liability if Commerce disagrees with the configuration.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Rural ORs are scrubbed in ninety minutes and Noah Feld holds the kill switch authority contractually. Noah Feld must choose between disabling telemetry with surgeon flights or domestic-only routing with liability. What does Noah Feld do?
